MMA event
Bellator 291: Amosov vs. Storley 2
The poster for Bellator 291: Amosov vs. Storley 2
PromotionBellator MMA
DateFebruary 25, 2023 (2023-02-25)
Venue3Arena
CityDublin, Ireland
Event chronology
Bellator 290: Bader vs. Fedor 2 Bellator 291: Amosov vs. Storley 2 Bellator 292: Nurmagomedov vs. Henderson

Bellator 291: Amosov vs. Storley 2 (also known as Bellator Dublin) was a mixed martial arts event produced by Bellator MMA, that took place on February 25, 2023, at the 3Arena in Dublin, Ireland.[1][2]

Background

[edit]

On February 25, Bellator hosted an event in the 3Arena in Dublin, Ireland. The event, which was Bellator's tenth event in the nation, after first debuting there in 2016. The promotion stated that the most recent event broke the 3Arena's attendance record.[1]

A Bellator Welterweight World Championship unification bout between current champion Yaroslav Amosov and interim champion Logan Storley headlined the event.[3] The pair previously met at Bellator 252, where Amosov won via split decision.[4]

A featherweight bout between Ciarán Clarke and Keir Harvie was scheduled for this event.[5] However, Harvie pulled out of the bout due to injury two weeks before the event and was replaced by Leonardos Sinis at a catchweight of 147.5 pounds.[6]

A welterweight bout between Oliver Enkamp and Luca Poclit was scheduled for this event.[7] However, Enkamp had to pull out of the bout due to injury.[8]

At the weigh-ins, Leonardos Sinis weighed in at 148.8 pounds, 1.3 pounds over the 147.5 pounds fight limit. The bout proceeded at catchweight with Sinis being fined 20% of his purse, which went to his opponent Ciarán Clarke.[9]

Main Card (Showtime)
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Welterweight Yaroslav Amosov (c) def. Logan Storley (ic) Decision (unanimous) (50–45, 50–45, 50–45) 5 5:00 [a]
Featherweight Jeremy Kennedy def. Pedro Carvalho Decision (unanimous) (30–27, 30–27, 30–27) 3 5:00
Lightweight Bryce Logan def. Peter Queally TKO (elbow and punches) 2 2:31
Women's Featherweight Sinead Kavanagh def. Janay Harding Decision (unanimous) (30–27, 29–28, 29–28) 3 5:00
Catchweight (148.8 lb) Ciarán Clarke def. Leonardos Sinis Decision (unanimous) (30–26, 30–26, 30–26) 3 5:00 [b]
Preliminary card (Youtube/Pluto TV)
Light Heavyweight Karl Moore def. Maciej Różański Decision (unanimous) (29–28, 29–28, 29–28) 3 5:00
Middleweight Mike Shipman def. Charlie Ward Decision (unanimous) (30–27, 30–27, 30–27) 3 5:00
Heavyweight Oleg Popov def. Gökhan Saricam Decision (unanimous) (29–28, 29–28, 29–28) 3 5:00
Middleweight Norbert Novenyi Jr. def. Andy Manzolo TKO (punch) 1 3:12
Bantamweight Brian Moore def. Luca Iovine Decision (split) (29–28, 28–29, 29–28) 3 5:00
Featherweight Richie Smullen def. Piotr Niedzielski Decision (split) (29–28, 28–29, 29–28) 3 5:00
Featherweight Darragh Kelly def. Dorval Jordan TKO (punches) 3 1:58
Featherweight Asaël Adjoudj def. Liam McCracken Decision (unanimous) (29–28, 29–28, 29–28) 3 5:00
Featherweight Khasan Magomedsharipov def. Rafael Hudson TKO (punches) 1 4:40
Women's Flyweight Jena Bishop def. Elina Kallionidou Decision (unanimous) (30–27, 30–27, 30–27) 3 5:00
Featherweight Kenny Mokhonoana def. Craig McIntosh TKO (elbows and punches) 1 3:13
Catchweight (165 lb) Dmytrii Hrytsenko def. Daniele Scatizzi Decision (unanimous) (30–27, 29–28, 29–28) 3 5:00
Welterweight Steven Hill def. Joël Kouadja Submission (rear-naked choke) 2 2:03
  1. ^ For the Bellator Welterweight World Championship.
  2. ^ Originally a 147.5 lb bout; Sinis missed weight (148.8 lb).

[10]
